action: In the open Google Chrome browser, click on the address bar, type 'https://www.facebook.com', and press Enter to navigate to Facebook.
fallback_action: Perform a Google search using Internet Explorer

recipe.steps: The task is to open Facebook in the Google Chrome browser. On the desktop, there is an icon labeled 'Google Chrome web browser' (Box ID 68). Since the instruction says 'in the open Google Chrome browser', I need to check if Chrome is already open. There is no visible Chrome window in the screenshot, so I will open Chrome by double-clicking its icon.

Google Chrome is now open, as seen in the new screenshot. To proceed with the task, I need to click on the address bar to enter the Facebook URL. The address bar is typically at the top of the Chrome window, but there is no bounding box directly labeled as the address bar. However, the most likely clickable area to focus the address bar is the top part of the Chrome window. Since there is no bounding box for the address bar, I will click on the Google Chrome window to bring it into focus, which should allow me to type the URL.

Google Chrome is now open and in focus. The next step is to click on the address bar to enter the Facebook URL. The bounding box labeled 'Google Chrome' (Box ID 7) is likely near the address bar area. Clicking here should allow me to type the URL.

The Chrome browser is open and in focus. The address bar is now likely active, as indicated by the cursor in the address bar area. The next step is to type the Facebook URL 'https://www.facebook.com' into the address bar as per the instructions.
